recalls.Description:
Johnson Health Tech has expanded its recall to include specific models of Matrix Retail and Vision treadmills due to a potential fire hazard. The affected models include Matrix T30, TF30, T50, TF50, T75, and Vision T9800, TF20, T40, and T80, which were manufactured between June 2007 and January 2024. These treadmills feature a running platform measuring 60 inches by 20 inches and are identifiable by the model and serial numbers located on the lower front crossbar near the power switch.

The issue arises from a power cord that may become loose from the treadmill's power socket, creating a fire risk. Previously recalled models were addressed with a power cord bracket that required tools for installation. However, this recall offers a simpler solution: consumers will receive a replacement power cord with an automatic locking-style plug that does not need tools for installation.
recalls.Title: Expanded Recall of Matrix and Vision Treadmills Due to Fire Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For more information, consumers can reach Johnson Health Tech North America at 800-211-4590 from 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. CT, Monday through Friday, email retailpowercord@johnsonfit.com, or visit https://matrixhomefitness.com/recalls or https://www.visionfitness.com/eng/recalls and click on 'Recalls'.

Recalled Matrix Retail TF30 (TM693, TM693B) Treadmill
Recalled Matrix Retail T50 (TM695, TM767) Treadmill
Recalled Matrix Retail TF50 Treadmill (TM694, TM694B, TM768)
Recalled Matrix Retail T75 (TM703, TM703B, TM703C, TM703E) Treadmill

Recalled Vision TF20 (TM432, TM432C) Treadmill
Recalled Vision T40 (TM433, TM433C) Treadmill
Recalled Vision T80 (TM445) Treadmill
Location of model and serial number on lower front crossbar

Injuries.Name: The firm has received 27 reports of overheating at the power cord-socket connection. No injuries have been reported.

Importers.Name: Johnson Health Tech Trading, Inc., of Cottage Grove, Wisconsin

Hazards.Name: Loose power cord from treadmill's power socket creating a fire hazard

Remedies.Name: Inspection of treadmills for power cord bracket; free replacement power cord with automatic locking plug for self-installation

recalls.metaDescription: Expanded recall of Matrix and Vision treadmills due to fire hazard. Check your model and contact for a free replacement power cord.

recalls.Description: The recall pertains to the AMP MP2 Smart Fitness Machine, a wall-mounted strength-training device designed for comprehensive resistance workouts. This model features an adjustable arm and connects to the Amp Fit app for tailored fitness routines. However, a safety issue has been identified that could lead to serious injuries. The arm may not lock securely, which can result in unexpected swinging, creating a risk of laceration.
recalls.Title: Amp Fit Recalls AMP MP2 Smart Fitness Machine Due to Laceration Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: Consumers should cease using the recalled fitness machine immediately and reach out to Amp Fit at 888-708-9560, available from Monday to Sunday, 9 a.m. to 9 p.m. CT. They can also email support@ampfit.com or visit http://joinamp.com/blog/product-safety-recall for further details.

Images.Caption: Recalled MP2 Model of Amp Smart Fitness Machine
Recalled MP2 Model of Amp Smart Fitness Machine

Injuries.Name: The firm has received 10 reports of the arm on the fitness machine not locking properly, resulting in lacerations to the head and bruising to the shoulder and other body.

Retailers.Name: Available online at Ampfit.com from January 2025 to August 2025 for approximately $1,800

Importers.Name: Amp Fit, Inc., of Middletown, Delaware

Hazards.Name: Risk of laceration or serious injury due to improper locking of the arm

Remedies.Name: Free inspection and repair by an authorized technician at the consumer's residence

recalls.metaDescription: Amp Fit recalls AMP MP2 Smart Fitness Machine due to laceration hazards. Stop using immediately and contact for free repairs.

recalls.Description: iFIT has announced a recall of NordicTrack Rowing Machines identified by model numbers NTRW19147.0, NTRW19147.1, NTRW19147.2, and NTRW19147.3. These models feature a black, gray, and white color scheme, with 'NordicTrack' displayed in black on the front legs and 'RW900' in white on the back legs. The model number can be found on a decal located on the rower base.
recalls.Title: iFIT Recalls NordicTrack Rowing Machines Due to Fire Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: Consumers are urged to stop using the rowing machines immediately and unplug them. For further assistance, contact iFIT at 833-680-4348 from 6 a.m. to 7 p.m. MT, Monday through Friday, email ntrw19147-recall@ifit.com, or visit www.nordictrack.com/product-recalls or www.nordictrack.com and click on 'Voluntary Recalls' at the bottom of the page.

Products.Name: NordicTrack Rowing Machine Model NTRW19147
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 44,800 (In addition, about 700 were sold in Canada)

Images.Caption: Recalled NordicTrack RW900 Rowing Machine

Injuries.Name: There have been eight reported incidents including two reports of fire, six reports of smoking or melting, and at least $6,000 in property damage. No injuries have been reported.

Retailers.Name: Available in-store and online at Best Buy, Dick's Sporting Goods, Nebraska Furniture, and ABT stores nationwide, as well as online at nordictrack.com and Amazon.com from November 2018 through April 2022 for approximately $1,700.

Hazards.Name: Overheating screen console posing a fire risk

Remedies.Name: Free home repair by an authorized technician, including a replacement screen console

recalls.metaDescription: iFIT recalls NordicTrack rowing machines due to fire hazards. Contact iFIT for a free repair or replacement.

recalls.Description: Johnson Health Tech Trading is recalling BowFlex Model 552 and Model 1090 Adjustable Dumbbells due to a potential impact hazard. The affected dumbbells, sold in pairs or individually, come in black and include handles, weight plates, and a molded plastic tray. The Model 552 adjusts from 5 to 52.5 pounds, while the Model 1090 adjusts from 10 to 90 pounds using manual knobs. The model and serial numbers can be found on a sticker on the bottom of the tray. The recall includes specific serial number ranges for both models.
recalls.Title: Recall of BowFlex Adjustable Dumbbells Due to Impact Hazard Affecting 3.7 Million Units
recalls.ConsumerContact: For more information, consumers can reach Johnson Health Tech Trading at 800-209-3539 from 8 a.m. to 7 p.m. CT, Monday through Friday, email recall@bowflex.com, or visit www.bowflex.com/recalls.

Images.Caption: Recalled BowFlex 552 52.5 LB Adjustable Dumbbell
Recalled BowFlex 552 52.5 LB Adjustable Dumbbell serial number location
Recalled BowFlex 1090 90 LB Adjustable Dumbbell
Recalled BowFlex 1090 90 LB Adjustable Dumbbell serial number location

Injuries.Name: Johnson Health Tech Trading has received 12 reports of the plates dislodging during use with no injuries for units it sold. Nautilus received 337 reports of the plates dislodging during use for units it sold, including 111 resulting in injuries such as concussions, abrasions, broken toes or contusions.

Retailers.Name: Johnson Fitness & Wellness, DICK'S Sporting Goods, Best Buy, and online at www.Bowflex.com and www.Amazon.com

Importers.Name: BowFlex Inc., (formerly Nautilus Inc.), of Vancouver, Washington
Johnson Health Tech Trading Inc., of Cottage Grove, Wisconsin

Hazards.Name: Dislodging weight plates posing an impact hazard

Remedies.Name: Refund or replacement options for recalled dumbbells

recalls.metaDescription: Recall of BowFlex Adjustable Dumbbells due to safety risks. Contact Johnson Health Tech Trading for refunds or replacements.

recalls.Description: Tzumi Electronics is recalling the FitRx SmartBell XL Quick-Select Adjustable Dumbbells, which range from 10 to 90 pounds (Model 9034, Serial Number KK23289034). These dumbbells feature a black design with red accents, and come with handles, weight plates, and a molded plastic storage tray. Users can adjust the weight in five-pound increments by turning the handle while the dumbbell is in the storage tray. The model and serial numbers are printed on the side of the tray, with 'FitRx' displayed on both the tray and the handles.
recalls.Title: Tzumi Electronics Recalls FitRx SmartBell XL Adjustable Dumbbells Due to Injury Risk; Sold Exclusively Online at Walmart
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Tzumi Electronics at 866-363-2237 from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, email XLRecall@tzumi.com, or visit www.myfitrx.com/Recall or www.fitrxrecovery.com and click on 'RECALL' at the bottom of the page for further details.

Products.Name: FitRx SmartBell XL Adjustable Dumbbells (10-90 lbs.)

Images.Caption: Recalled FitRx SmartBell XL, Quick-Select Adjustable Dumbbell (10-90 lbs.), Model 9034
Location of model number and serial number on the side of the unit's storage tray

Injuries.Name: There have been more than 60 reports of weight plates dislodging during use, which have been linked to at least seven injuries, including bruises, contusions and abrasions.

Retailers.Name: Available exclusively online at www.walmart.com from September 2023 through June 2024, priced between $150 and $200.

Importers.Name: Tzumi Electronics Inc., of New York

Hazards.Name: Risk of weight plates dislodging from the dumbbell handle during use, leading to potential injury.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should cease using the recalled dumbbells immediately and contact Tzumi Electronics for a complimentary repair that includes a new dumbbell handle and storage tray.

recalls.metaDescription: Tzumi recalls FitRx SmartBell XL Adjustable Dumbbells due to injury risk. Contact for free repairs and stop using immediately.

recalls.Description: Johnson Health Tech North America has issued a recall for specific models of Matrix-brand Training Cycles, including CXP-03, CXC-02, CXM-03, CXV, and CXM-02. These cycles feature adjustable saddles and handlebars, an aluminum rear flywheel, and a magnetic resistance system, all housed in a matte black steel frame. The model and serial numbers can be found on a label located on the lower rear section of the cycle frame. Recalled cycles will have serial numbers beginning with the prefixes FC32, ZFC32B, FC33, FC36, FC29D, or FC27, followed by additional digits.
recalls.Title: Recall of Matrix-Brand Training Cycles by Johnson Health Tech North America Due to Fall Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers should reach out to Johnson Health Tech North America Inc. at their toll-free number 866-218-3674, available from 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. CT, Monday through Friday. They can also email cxrecall@matrixfitness.com or visit https://www.matrixfitness.com/us/eng/recalls for further details. The company is proactively contacting all known purchasers.

Products.Name: Matrix CXP-03 Training Cycle
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 12,885 (In addition, about 140 were sold in Canada)

Images.Caption: Recalled Matrix-brand Training Cycle Model CXC-02
Recalled Matrix-brand Training Cycle Model CXM-02, Model CXM-03
Recalled Matrix-brand Training Cycle Model CXP-03
Recalled Matrix-brand Training Cycle Model CXV
Location of Serial Number on Rear of Cycle Frame

Injuries.Name: The firm has received 63 reports of seats unexpectedly lowering, including two reports that users fell off the cycle when the seat lowered. No injuries have been reported.

Retailers.Name: Primarily distributed to commercial fitness centers by JHTNA and third-party distributors from January 2021 to October 2024, with prices ranging from $2,530 to $7,305.

Importers.Name: Johnson Health Tech North America Inc. ("JHTNA"), of Cottage Grove, Wisconsin

Hazards.Name: Risk of falling due to unexpected lowering of the adjustable seat during use.

Remedies.Name: Consumers must cease using the recalled cycles and contact Johnson Health Tech North America Inc. to arrange for a technician to install a complimentary repair kit.

recalls.metaDescription: Johnson Health Tech recalls Matrix-brand Training Cycles due to a fall hazard. Immediate action required for safety.

recalls.Description: Precor is recalling its Resolute Cable Multi-Station exercise equipment, Model No. RMS905, which was distributed to gyms across the country. This equipment features an adjustable pulley carriage designed for cable resistance exercises, allowing users to adjust the height along a vertical rail. The Precor brand name is displayed above the vertical rail, while the model number can be found on a label under the pulley assembly's top frame, situated between the vertical rail and the weight stack. The dimensions of the equipment are 68 inches by 58 inches by 94 inches, and it is available in silver, black, or white frames with various upholstery options.
recalls.Title: Precor Recalls Resolute™ Cable Multi-Station Exercise Equipment Due to Risk of Impact Injuries
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Precor Incorporated at 800-347-4404, via email at support@precor.com, or visit www.precor.com/recall. Additional information can be found by clicking on "RECALL INFORMATION" at the top of the homepage.

Products.Name: Precor Resolute Cable Multi-Station Exercise Equipment

Images.Caption: Recalled Resolute Cable Multi-Station

Injuries.Name: Precor has received nine reports of the pulley dropping during use, including two minor head injuries.

Retailers.Name: This equipment was sold to fitness facilities nationwide from July 2023 to October 2024, priced between $3,020 and $3,340.

Hazards.Name: Potential for the pop-pin to fail, leading to the pulley carriage dropping unexpectedly and causing impact injuries.

Remedies.Name: Consumers must cease using the recalled equipment immediately. Precor will contact all affected gyms to arrange a complimentary on-site repair by a qualified technician, which includes installing a redesigned pop-pin and a new warning label.

recalls.metaDescription: Precor recalls Resolute Cable Multi-Station exercise equipment due to injury risks. Immediate action recommended for users.

recalls.Description: The recall pertains to the ProForm Rapid Strike 50 LB Adjustable Dumbbell Set, model PAMSDB20. These dumbbells, sold in black, feature adjustable weights from 5 to 50 pounds using a manual selection pin, and come with handles, weight plates, and a molded plastic tray. The model number can be found on a sticker located under the lip of the tray's base.
recalls.Title: iFIT Recalls ProForm 50 LB Adjustable Dumbbells Due to Injury Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach iFIT at 833-680-4348 from 6 a.m. to 6 p.m. MT, Monday through Friday, email pamsdb20-recall@ifit.com, or visit www.proform.com/product-recalls for further details. Additional information is available at www.proform.com by selecting 'Voluntary Recall' at the bottom of the page.

Products.Name: ProForm Rapid Strike 50 LB Adjustable Dumbbell Set
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 54,400 (In addition, about 650 sold in Canada)

Images.Caption: Recalled iFIT ProForm Rapid Strike 50 LB Adjustable Dumbbell Set
Recalled iFIT ProForm Rapid Strike 50 LB Adjustable Dumbbell Set
Recalled iFIT ProForm Rapid Strike 50 LB Adjustable Dumbbell Set

Injuries.Name: The firm has received eight reports of weight plates dislodging during use with three resulting in injuries, including contusions and abrasions.

Retailers.Name: Available at ProForm.com, Amazon.com, DicksSportingGoods.com, Lowes.com, and Walmart.com from February 2021 through May 2024, priced around $350.

Importers.Name: iFIT Inc., f/k/a Icon Health & Fitness, of Logan, Utah

Hazards.Name: Risk of weight plates dislodging from the handle during use, leading to potential impact injuries.

Remedies.Name: Consumers are advised to cease use of the recalled dumbbells immediately and contact iFIT for a free replacement of the molded tray. iFIT will reach out to all known purchasers directly.

recalls.metaDescription: iFIT recalls ProForm 50 LB Adjustable Dumbbells due to injury risks. Learn how to get a replacement and stay safe.

recalls.Description: Johnson Health Tech North America has expanded its recall to include Matrix Fitness T1xe, T3x, and T3xm commercial treadmills. These treadmills feature a running platform measuring 60 inches in length and 20 inches in width, along with a 33-inch-wide console. The treadmills are identifiable by the Matrix branding on the console masts, and the model name and serial number can be found on the connecting bar near the power socket. The affected units were manufactured between March 2018 and January 2024.
recalls.Title: Expanded Recall of Matrix T1xe, T3x, and T3xm Commercial Treadmills Due to Fire Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Johnson Health Tech North America at 866-218-3674 from 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. CT, Monday through Friday, email reworks@johnsonfit.com, or visit https://matrixfitness.com/us/eng/recalls for more details.

Products.Name: Matrix T1xe Treadmill
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 9,700 (About 19,900 were previously recalled in January 2022)

Images.Caption: Recalled Matrix T3xe Treadmill
Recalled Matrix T3xm Treadmill
Recalled Matrix T1xe MAX Treadmill
Location on recalled treadmill of serial number
Location of model name and the serial number on the connecting bar at the bottom of the treadmill

Injuries.Name: Matrix Fitness has received one report of a fire in a fitness facility since the last recall, in addition to the 58 reported in the January 2022 recall, of the treadmill's power cord and socket sparking, smoking, and/or melting, including seven reports of fire; two resulting in property damage. No injuries have been reported.

Retailers.Name: Available at Johnson Health Tech North America and Johnson Fitness & Wellness stores across the country from March 2018 to January 2024, priced between $3,400 and $8,900.

Importers.Name: Johnson Health Tech North America Inc., of Cottage Grove, Wis.

Hazards.Name: Loose power cord creating a fire hazard

Remedies.Name: Inspect treadmills for power cord bracket installation and ensure proper cord insertion; contact for free bracket installation.

recalls.metaDescription: Matrix T1xe, T3x, and T3xm treadmills recalled due to fire hazards; contact Johnson Health Tech for safety measures.

recalls.Description:
The recall pertains to the iFIT NordicTrack 50 LB iSelect Adjustable Dumbbells, model NTSAW23020.0. These dumbbells, available in black, come with handles, weight plates, a tablet holder, and a base. They feature an electronic motorized weight selector that allows adjustments from 5 to 50 pounds in 5-pound increments, responding to voice commands or manual knob adjustments. Additionally, the dumbbells include Bluetooth connectivity to sync with the iFIT app for workout guidance and data tracking.

Consumers are advised to cease using the recalled dumbbells immediately due to a safety concern where the weight plates may detach from the handle during use, leading to potential impact hazards. iFIT is proactively reaching out to all known buyers to address this issue.
recalls.Title: iFIT Recalls NordicTrack 50 LB iSelect Adjustable Dumbbells Due to Safety Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For more information, contact iFIT at 833-680-4348 from 6 a.m. to 6 p.m. MT, Monday through Friday, or email iselect-recall@ifit.com. Visit www.nordictrack.com/product-recalls or go to www.nordictrack.com and click on 'Voluntary Recall' at the bottom of the page.

Products.Name: NordicTrack 50 LB iSelect Adjustable Dumbbells
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 15,400 (In addition, about 650 sold in Canada)

Images.Caption: Recalled iFIT NordicTrack 50 LB iSelect Voice-Controlled Adjustable Dumbbells

Injuries.Name: There were 40 reports of weight plates dislodging during use with eight resulting in injuries such as contusions and abrasions.

Retailers.Name: Available at NordicTrack.com, Amazon.com, and DicksSportingGoods.com between January 2022 and November 2023, priced between $500 and $600.

Importers.Name: iFIT Inc., f/k/a Icon Health & Fitness, of Logan, Utah

Hazards.Name: Risk of weight plates dislodging from the handle during use

Remedies.Name: Consumers should stop using the dumbbells and contact iFIT for a full refund. iFIT will reach out to all known purchasers.

recalls.metaDescription: iFIT recalls NordicTrack 50 LB iSelect Adjustable Dumbbells due to safety concerns. Stop using immediately and contact for a refund.
